metadata.title: Philadelphia City All-Star Football Game Database
metadata.description: Comprehensive historical records of Public vs. Non-Public All-Star games (1975-2019)
metadata.source: Compiled from OCR, text scraping, and historical records

scoring_summary: Public-Safery (Nedley and Tuck tackled Daley in end zone)
games.notes: Parochial-Morrin 41 pass from Maley (Sheridan extra point) - Parochial-Citrone 8 run (Kick failed) - Public-Murphy 5 run (run failed)
games.highlights: Overflow crowd of 14,426 attended. Jack Maley (Penn Charter) passed 3-for-6 for 79 yards and first TD in All-Star history, a 41-yard pass to Mike Morrin (North Catholic). John Citrone (Roman) scored on 8-yard run.

games.notes: Pub-Benson 1 run (Freiling kick); Par-Kearns 10 pass from McKay (pass failed); Pub-Montgomery 7 run (Freiling kick)
games.highlights: Stellar defense limited Non-Public to minus-14 yards total offense. Nate Montgomery (Lincoln) rushed 14 times for 53 yards and clinching TD with 43 seconds left. Bob Keels (Central) made three catches for 37 yards.

scoring_summary: P - McClelland 15 run (White pass from Hodges)
NP - Lafferty 17 pass from Paulone (Smith pass from Paulone)
games.officials: Tom (Goalpost) Elliott, Charles Allen, Bob Bram, Ralph McNally, Frank Monaco, Paul Baker
games.notes: P - McClelland 15 run (White pass from Hodges); NP - Lafferty 17 pass from Paulone (Smith pass from Paulone)
games.highlights: Non-Public tied game in fourth quarter. Mike Paulone (Pennsylvania School for the Deaf) completed 17-yard TD pass to Paul Lafferty (Judge) and conversion to Jerry Smith (Neumann). Leo McClelland (Mastbaum) rushed 19 times for 85 yards.

scoring_summary: NP - Gramlich 29 pass from Clark (Bushheck kick)
NP - Wistar 7 pass from Loughery (Bushheck kick)
NP - Gramlich 14 pass from Loughery (Bushheck kick)
NP - Powers 42 pass from Loscalzo (Bushheck kick)
games.notes: Public: Derek Coleman (West, 1 completion-8 attempts, 35 yards); Non-Public: Ed Clark (Judge, 1 completion-3 attempts, 29 yards); Bob Loscaito (Dougherty, 4-1-6 carries-yards)
games.highlights: 28,432 fans attended. John Loughery (Penn Charter) completed 3-for-4 for 72 yards and two TDs. Tim Gramlich (Roman) had two catches for 78 yards and two TDs. Brian Ewell (Chestnut Hill) rushed 13 times for 79 yards and TD.

scoring_summary: P - Taylor 18 pass from Speller (pass failed)
games.notes: NP - Ulmer 2 run (Cowley kick); P - Taylor 18 pass from Speller (pass failed); NP - McIneman 2 run (Cowley kick)
games.highlights: Harry Ulmer (North Catholic) rushed 14 times for 138 yards and TD (rushing yardage record). Joe McErlean (SJ Prep) ran for other TD. Don Speller (Bartram) threw 18-yard TD pass.

scoring_summary: P - Twyman 3 pass from Ingram (gram kick failed)
games.notes: NP - J. Pawloski 29 pass from (kick); P - Crawford 10 run (Single kick); P - Twyman 3 pass from Ingram (gram kick failed)
games.highlights: Robert Crawford (Central) ran 10 yards for TD. Kevin Ingram (Dobbins) passed 3 yards to Alan Twyman (Central) for TD. Karl Siegle (Northeast) kicked PAT. Jim Pawloski (Ryan) made two receptions for 74 yards.

games.highlights: Game played in driving rainstorm. Bob Hudoka (Dougherty) kicked 26-yard field goal. John McGeehan (Roman) ran 2 yards for TD. Jay Curcio (Penn Charter) intercepted; Tim Dronson recovered fumble.

games.highlights: Ultra-windy conditions affected play. Charles Coyle (Dougherty) made two interceptions. Jerry Magallanes (Judge) rushed 12 times for 36 yards and two TDs. Public's rushing defense netted minus-9 yards.

games.highlights: Steve Duncan (Germantown) rushed 27 times for 107 yards. Charles Thomas (Frankford) rushed nine times for 112 yards with 90-yard TD. Kenny Young (King) passed 8-for-17 for 91 yards and score.

games.highlights: Cary Boyd (Northeast) ran for one TD and passed for another. Dave Parkinson (Northeast) kicked 32 and 35-yard field goals. Blair Thomas (Frankford) rushed 11 times for 48 yards and TD.

games.highlights: Ron Bryant (King) caught 2-yard TD pass from Keith Singleton (Washington) with 2:23 left breaking 6-6 tie. Todd Mitchell (King) rushed 20 times for 97 yards. Kenyatta Rush (Central) led defense with eight tackles.

Al Settembrino (Ryan) rushed 26 times for 114 yards and two TDs. Tim Cunniff (Ryan) kicked decisive 20-yard field goal with 3:28 remaining. Rich Ritchie (Roman) forced fumble on Public's final possession.

First All-Star game played at night. Jerome Bailey (Bok) scored only TD on 1-yard run 2:57 before halftime. Harun Reed (Frankford) recorded 10 tackles and interception. Roger Talley (Dobbins) made three interceptions.

Jeff Simmins (Judge) rushed nine times for 56 yards and two TDs despite seasonal high of 50 yards. Frank Wycheck (Ryan) added 68 yards and TD on 11 carries. Patrick Tomosky (Roxborough) passed 5-for-10 for 129 yards.

Public scored first 13 points. Charles Way (Northeast) made seven tackles to key defense forcing five turnovers. Dave MacDonald (Lincoln) passed for two TDs. Frank Costa (SJ Prep) completed 10-for-20 for 83 yards.

Two Non-Public TDs came on defense. Darrin MacMillan (Dougherty) recovered fumble in end zone; Mike Conway (Dougherty) ran 7 yards with fumble for TD. Mica Dawkins (Gratz) scored on 89-yard kickoff return for Public.

Scotty Mack (Roxborough) rushed nine times for 116 yards and two TDs (20 and 56 yards). Non-Public rushing attack netted minus-9 yards on 30 carries. Stacey Lane (Olney) led defense with seven tackles.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox93.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: First All-Star game played in May (previous 18 in April). Brian Kennedy (Judge) scored winning 1-yard TD with 24 seconds remaining. Troy Kersey (Dougherty) caught six passes for 100 yards.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox94.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Jess Sodaski (SJ Prep) rushed nine times for 57 yards and TD, passed 4-for-7. Lance Shaw (SJ Prep) rushed 20 times for 100 yards and two TDs. Jonathan Perry (Gratz) made 11 tackles including four for losses.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox95.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Spencer Whetts (Germantown) scored first TD on 1-yard sneak and passed for another to Javer Dillard. Hassan Brockman (Dobbins) scored winning TD on 38-yard reverse with 3:20 left.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox96.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Tim Dumas (Ryan) converted holder-to-TD trick play, scampering 12 yards with 2:30 remaining from trailing 6-3. Scott Palanjian (SJ Prep) kicked 37-yard field goal. Chris Kennedy (Ryan) rushed 18 times for 69 yards.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox97.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: George Radu (Washington) set game record with three field goals (27, 37, 31 yards). Ed Giddings (University City) scored on 80-yard flanker reverse TD. Steve Szydlik (Frankford) passed 10-for-17 for 101 yards.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox98.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Jeff Moore (Ryan) scored winning 1-yard TD with 1:18 remaining. Mike Prince (Dougherty) made five receptions for 90 yards. Joe Corsanico (Ryan) passed 4-for-9 for 89 yards.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox99.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Game ended dramatically with defensive stop at 6-inch line on fourth-and-goal from the 2. Andre Davis (Dobbins) contributed 95-yard fumble return TD. Edvard Jean (Washington) threw TD pass to David Kenner.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstar00.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Ben Dever (Dougherty) and Kevin Stefanski (SJ Prep) combined for 10-for-18, 137 yards passing. Non-Public defense limited Public to 56 yards on 33 plays. Angel Moultair (Germantown) led with 19 tackles.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Public posted fourth series shutout. Teddy Lipford (Central) hit Jose Pagan (Edison) with 80-yard TD pass (record). Roger Price (Washington) kicked 44-yard field goal and earlier 22-yarder.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Non-Public rallied from 14-0 deficit. SJ Prep teammates Jim Lachman and Pete Chromiak caught TD passes. Justin O'Brien (Judge) kicked game-winning 31-yard field goal.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Pat Kaiser (SJ Prep) rushed 22 times for 75 yards and two TDs. Johnny Ortiz (Roman) scored TD on 3-yard pass from Matt Ryan (Penn Charter) with 20 seconds before halftime. Non-Public forced five turnovers.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
games.highlights: Curtis Brinkley (West Catholic) scored three TDs rushing, first All-Star player to do so. Andre Sloan-El (Roman) shattered passing yards record with 175 on 13-for-16. Game disrupted by gunshots in parking lot with 2:21 remaining.

games.notes: Jerry Butler (Washington) recorded 13.3 yards per carry with 3 rushing TDs
games.highlights: Public shutout Non-Public. Jerry Butler (Washington) rushed for record-tying three TDs including 79-yard score. Sammy Tranks (King) set record with 67-yard TD from interception return.

games.notes: Bill Edger (SJP) caught 6 passes for 128 yards
games.highlights: Bill Edger (SJ Prep) set receiving yardage record with 128 yards on six catches, including 42-yard TD. Mike Varanavage (Ryan) rushed 21 times for 103 yards and two TDs despite not expected to carry ball.

games.notes: Kendell Coleman (King) received 4 passes for 135 yards and recorded a 64-yard punt return TD
games.highlights: Public forced overtime with late TD, won on fourth-down run by Calvin Spires (Frankford). Kendell Coleman (King) had 215 yards on 14 touches with two TD runs. Jaelen Strong-Rankin broke receiving record with 237 yards for Non-Public.

games.notes: Mike Mattei (Chestnut Hill) passed for 3 TDs; Al Desiderio (Roman) caught 4 passes for 140 yards with 2 TD receptions
games.highlights: Easiest victory in all-star history. Al Desiderio (Roman) set receiving yardage record with 140 yards on four catches. Mike Mattei (Chestnut Hill) passed 7-for-9 for 148 yards and three TDs.

games.scoring_summary: Non-Public dominated 40-0 with first six possessions producing TDs. Mercy rule invoked with 7:34 remaining in 3Q.
games.notes: Judge players left at halftime for prom after 33-0 lead established.
games.highlights: Non-Public dominated with shutout; mercy rule invoked with 7:34 remaining in third quarter. First six possessions produced TDs. Judge players left at halftime for prom after 33-0 lead established.

games.scoring_summary: Non-Public won 16-15. Will Huff tied FG record with 3 makes (27, 28, 33 yds). Attendance: 4,936.
games.notes: First meeting at Lincoln Financial Field with 4,936 attendance.
games.highlights: First meeting at Lincoln Financial Field with 4,936 attendance. Will Huff (Neumann-Goretti) tied field goal record with three makes and game-winning PAT. Pete Hurley (SJ Prep) caught 17-yard TD pass.

games.scoring_summary: Public reversed 20-16 deficit with two INT return TDs in final 8 minutes. Camille Max 51-yd pick-6; Daquan Cooper 95-yd return TD.
games.notes: Public achieved major comeback with defensive scores.
games.highlights: Public reversed 20-16 deficit with two interception return TDs in final eight minutes. Camille Max (Northeast) scored 51-yard pick six; Daquan Cooper (Washington) added 95-yard return TD. Jaelen Strong-Rankin (West Catholic) set receiving record with 237 yards.

games.scoring_summary: Public won 29-22. Sharif Smith scored two 1-yard TDs. Multiple special rules violations.
games.notes: Public achieved second consecutive win. Shakur NeSmith ejected in Q2 but reinstated at halftime.
games.highlights: Public achieved second consecutive win. Sharif Smith (Furness) scored two 1-yard TDs. Shakur NeSmith ejected in second quarter but reinstated at halftime following coach agreement. Game featured multiple special rules violations.

Public claimed third consecutive win. Vittorio Goggins (Bok) recorded 15 tackles with five for loss. Antoine Whitney (Bok) scored 79-yard TD on lateral kickoff return. Non-Public suited only 28 players.

Non-Public allowed suburban Catholic school players for first time; newcomers accounted for all five TDs. Joseph Walker (King) set passing yards record with 183 despite four interceptions. Game had 27 penalties for 265 yards.

Dramatic finish with Non-Public TD with 0.8 seconds remaining. Paul Dooley (SCH Academy) hit John Hall (Wood) for TD; 35-yard PAT succeeded after special violations penalty. Alex Arcangeli (Wood) broke rushing yardage record with 193 yards.

games.mvp: James Gillespie (offense), Gianni Cruel (defense), Anthony Russo (sportsmanship)
games.highlights: Public scored record-tying 58 points. Shayne Smith (Del-Val) passed for 305 yards and three TDs. DeJason Ellis (Mastery North) set PAT record with seven conversions. Game featured 857 scrimmage yards.

Non-Public built 19-point third quarter lead after slim halftime advantage. Three consecutive TDs in third quarter all covered 34 yards. Nasir Boykin (Imhotep) passed 11-for-27 for 238 yards for Public.

Public outscored Non-Public 26-0 in final three quarters. Mahmud Dioubate (Bartram) tied record with three passing TDs on 6-for-12, 104 yards. Talid Crippen (Mastery North) scored on unusual fumble return TD spanning 47 yards.

Public won with a two-point conversion in final 45 seconds. Tarik Bey (Bartram) scored on QB sneak; Jordan Price (Frankford) added winning conversion. Bey completed 9-for-16 for 108 yards and 28-yard TD pass to Yusuf Terry (Imhotep).
